dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3416 IS: Safeguarding U.S. Rulemaking Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Lummis (for herself, Mr. Ricketts, Mr. Grassley, and Mr. Sheehy) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To prohibit public comments from governments and individuals designated as foreign adversaries.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Safeguarding U.S. Rulemaking Act.

section.header: Limiting public comment to U.S. citizens and entities
section.text: Section 553 of title 5, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (c), by striking After notice and inserting Except as provided in subsection (f), after notice;
in subsection (e), by striking Each agency and inserting Except as provided in subsection (f), each agency; and
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: A foreign government that is determined by the Secretary of Commerce to be a foreign adversary under section 791.4(a) of title 15, Code of Federal Regulations, or a national of or entity incorporated in such foreign government shall be ineligible to participate in rule making or petition an agency under this section.
